interactions.summary: The interviewer notes the flood of people wanting access to Jiang and calls the smear campaign against him ineffective, and Jiang answers that the attacks show his opponents are desperate and anxious.
The interviewer asks what to expect from Predictive History and from Jiang personally in the future.

Jiang says he wants to systematize his Kabbalah and war-of-spirit material for a general audience, may take time to write a game-theory book, and hopes eventually to build hopeful institutions like a school or community.

The interviewer invites Jiang back, Jiang agrees in principle to another appearance about a month later around April 8, and the interviewer thanks him while asking for any final thought for the audience.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interviewer invitation, Jiang acceptance, interviewer thanks

speaker_notes.note: The ASR compresses the phrase into something like 'write a book by game theory,' but Jiang's meaning is clear: he wants to write a book about game theory.

claims.claim: The interviewer says the coordinated character-assassination campaign against Jiang failed and that many public figures now want to speak with him.
Jiang says the attacks on him reveal how desperate and anxious his opponents are.
Jiang says he wants to systematize his Kabbalah and war-of-spirit discussions and present them more clearly to a general lecture audience.
Jiang says he may take a few months off to write a book about game theory so that people feel more empowered to understand global events.
Jiang says he wants his future work to give people hope, possibly by starting a school or community, because showing only corruption and evil can drive listeners into despair.
claims.temporal_scope: Institutional and moral ambition stated on 2026-03-09.

claims.claim: Jiang tentatively agrees to another conversation about a month later, naming April 8 as a rough target date.
claims.temporal_scope: Scheduling statement made on 2026-03-09 about a future interview.

claims.claim: The interviewer closes by thanking Jiang for fighting for what is good and by asking for any final thought he wants to leave with the audience.

signature_moments.moment: The smear campaign becomes evidence of elite panic rather than of Jiang's isolation.
signature_moments.source_phrase: it just shows you how desperate and anxious they are
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Jiang turns attempted delegitimation into a diagnostic reading of regime fear.

signature_moments.moment: The analyst of corruption says he now wants to become a source of light and hope.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I want to be a source of light
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the packet's key turn from diagnosis into institution-building and positive vocation.

signature_moments.moment: A game-theory book is imagined as a way to make ordinary people feel empowered by world events rather than crushed by them.
signature_moments.source_phrase: people feel more empowered to understand global events
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This line links Jiang's intellectual method to a concrete educational mission.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang refers back to this phrase as material he now wants to systematize for a broader audience.
Jiang's self-description for the role he wants his future projects to play against despair and corruption.
Jiang's proposed future project for teaching people how to interpret global events through structured incentives and strategy.

chronology_notes.note: This packet closes the interview by shifting from Jiang's wartime analysis into his future teaching and institution-building plans, plus a concrete follow-up date around April 8.

uncertainty_notes.note: The future-project details are clear, but the exact phrasing around the book plan and the follow-up scheduling remains conversational and provisional rather than fully fixed.
